channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Sabalenka cuts news conference short as French Open players protest over pay
item.description: Leading players, including Aryna Sabalenka, Coco Gauff and Jannik Sinner, limit their French Open media duties to 15 minutes in a row over prize money.

item.title: Trust and a connection - why Raducanu rehired Richardson
item.description: Emma Raducanu believes it is "very difficult to say" she made a mistake in parting ways with recently rehired coach Andrew Richardson after her US Open triumph.

item.title: GB's Gill loses in final round of qualifying
item.description: Britain's Felix Gill will have to wait to make his Grand Slam singles debut after he fell at the final hurdle in French Open qualifying.

item.title: Raducanu faces tricky start - who plays who at French Open?
item.description: Emma Raducanu is handed a tricky test at the French Open after being drawn against Argentine clay-court specialist Solano Sierra in the first round.

item.title: From 1,945 to French Open main draw - GB's Samuel qualifies for first major
item.description: Britain's Toby Samuel reaches the first Grand Slam main draw of his career with victory in the final round of French Open qualifying.

item.title: Two-time winner Alcaraz out of Wimbledon with injury
item.description: Two-time winner Carlos Alcaraz will miss this year's Wimbledon because of a wrist injury.

item.title: 'I broke the curse' - the fall and rise of a teenage Grand Slam champion
item.description: When 19-year-old Bianca Andreescu stunned Serena Williams to win the US Open it was unthinkable it would be more than 2,300 days before her next triumph.

item.title: Players to cut short French Open interviews in pay row
item.description: The world's leading tennis players plan to limit their pre-French Open media commitments to 15 minutes in protest at the prize money on offer.

item.title: French Open - schedule, seedings and how to follow
item.description: The second Grand Slam of the year begins on Sunday as players head to Paris for the French Open - here's all you need to know.

item.title: Queen's women's prize money to increase by 35%
item.description: There will be a 35% increase in prize money for the women's tournament at Queen's Club in June.

item.title: Sinner speeds into history by completing 'Golden Masters'
item.description: Jannik Sinner wins the Italian Open in Rome to become only the second man in history - and easily the youngest - to claim all nine ATP Masters titles.

item.title: Raducanu loses on return after two months out
item.description: Emma Raducanu suffers a narrow first-round defeat in Strasbourg as she returns to action after more than two months away from the WTA Tour.

item.title: Bolton to step down as All England Club chief
item.description: Sally Bolton will step down as the All England Lawn Tennis Club's (AELTC) chief executive after Wimbledon following six years in the role.

item.title: Svitolina upsets Gauff to win Italian Open thriller
item.description: Ukraine's Elina Svitolina battles to a third Italian Open title courtesy of a thrilling three-set win against Coco Gauff in the final.

item.title: Raducanu reunited full-time with coach from US Open win
item.description: Emma Raducanu reunites with coach Andrew Richardson, who helped her to the US Open title as a teenage qualifier in 2021, on a full-time basis.

item.title: 'Disgraceful' vandalism of Eastbourne courts
item.description: Damage to several courts has been discovered at Devonshire Park – the home of the Eastbourne Open.

item.title: Murray brought into Draper coaching team
item.description: Britain's Jack Draper brings Andy Murray into his coaching team for the grass-court season after parting company with Jamie Delgado.

item.title: Who has won the most French Open titles?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team looks at who has won the most French Open singles and doubles titles.

item.title: Confident Swiatek rediscovers ruthless best in Rome
item.description: Four-time French Open champion Iga Swiatek produces another statement win to reach the Rome semi-finals - and is finding her form just in time for Roland Garros.

item.title: How impending retirement is fuelling Cirstea's success
item.description: Romania's Sorana Cirstea is playing some of the finest tennis in her 20-year career - will she be tempted to reverse her decision to retire at the end of 2026?

item.title: Djokovic stunned by qualifier on return to action in Rome
item.description: An out-of-sorts Novak Djokovic suffers defeat on his return to action as he is stunned by gutsy qualifier Dino Prizmic in the Italian Open second round.

item.title: Players not getting the respect they deserve - Sinner
item.description: Jannik Sinner admits it is "tough to say" whether he would ever boycott a Grand Slam over prize money, but claims players are not getting the respect they deserve.

item.title: 'No substitutes in tennis' - why are so many British players injured?
item.description: British tennis players have struggled with injuries throughout the clay-court season - but why?

item.title: Sinner completes 'Golden Masters' with win in Rome
item.description: Watch highlights as world number one Jannik Sinner beats Norway's Casper Ruud 6-4 6-4 to win the Italian Open and complete the full set of nine ATP Masters 1000 titles - known as the 'career Golden Masters'.

item.title: Seven-time Grand Slam champion Murray retires
item.description: Seven-time Grand Slam champion Jamie Murray retires from tennis as one of the most-decorated doubles players in the world. Here's a look back at his 2007 and 2017 mixed doubles triumphs at Wimbledon.

item.title: Murray retires after 'tough year of tennis'
item.description: Seven-time Grand Slam champion Jamie Murray retires from tennis, saying he had no desire to return to the sport and has no regrets.

item.title: That's for you Rory! - Alcaraz toasts win with golf celebration
item.description: Carlos Alcaraz beats Joao Fonseca in the Men's singles at the Miami Open, and celebrates with a golf swing in front on the spectating Rory McIllroy.

item.title: Draper cruises to Davis Cup win on return from injury
item.description: Jack Draper cruises to an emphatic victory on his long-awaited return from injury to give Great Britain the lead over Norway in Davis Cup qualifying.

item.title: Five sets for women? More entertainment? - where does 'Glastonbury of tennis' go next?
item.description: Once dubbed the 'Happy Slam' by Roger Federer, the innovative Australian Open has gone from strength to strength - but there have been signs of discontent.

item.title: Happy tennis, serious name - the making of Carlos Alcaraz
item.description: Before Carlos Alcaraz became the youngest man to complete the career Grand Slam, he was simply Carlitos from Murcia. This is his story.

item.title: 'She basically capitulated' - is Sabalenka underachieving at Slams?
item.description: After losing another Grand Slam final - this time to Elena Rybakina at the Australian Open - BBC Sport asks if Aryna Sabalenka is underachieving at the majors.

item.title: Surviving bombs and hunger to become a professional tennis player
item.description: Tennis is seen as a rich sport. But many stars, including Novak Djokovic and Elina Svitolina, have forged successful careers from humble beginnings.

item.title: Is Sinner-Alcaraz in danger of making majors boring?
item.description: Though the Carlos Alcaraz and Jannik Sinner rivalry is thrilling, tennis correspondent Russell Fuller ponders whether it could make Grand Slams boring.

item.title: Why Norrie exit underlines how Britain is punching below weight
item.description: Britain is one of the richest tennis nations in the world but once again its singles players have not reached the second week at a Grand Slam.

item.title: Players at 'high risk' as tennis chases sun
item.description: With high temperatures and very high UV levels at the Australian Open, BBC Sport looks at the effects of playing for hours outdoors.

item.title: 'I'm always the chaser?' - Djokovic unimpressed by reporter's question
item.description: 24-time Grand Slam winner Novak Djokovic says he felt "disrespectful" after being asked how he felt "chasing" Jannik Sinner and Carlos Alcaraz, after previously "chasing" Roger Federer and Rafael Nadal during the early parts of his career.

item.title: 'Are we animals in the zoo?' - Top tennis players call for more privacy
item.description: Jessica Pegula, Iga Swiatek and Novak Djokovic have all supported Coco Gauff's concerns over the use of cameras away from the court at the Australian Open, insisting more privacy is needed for all players competing.

item.title: 'I don't want to give myself too much of a hard time'
item.description: British number one Emma Raducanu says she is leaving the Australian Open with her "head held high" after losing 7-6 (7-3) 6-2 to Austria's Anastasia Potapova in the second round.

item.title: Watch: Amateur beats Sinner in One Point Slam title win
item.description: Watch amateur tennis player beat men's world number two Jannik Sinner and women's world number 117 Joanna Garland to earn A$1m (£496,835) in Australian Open's new Million Dollar One Point Slam.

item.title: Kyrgios defeats Sabalenka in 'Battle of the Sexes'
item.description: Watch the best moments between Aryna Sabalenka and Nick Kyrgios at the Battle of the Sexes in Dubai.

item.title: 'Point of the match!' - Sabalenka hits 'amazing' winner against Kyrgios
item.description: Aryna Sabalenka hits an 'amazing' return against Nick Kyrgios at the Battle of the Sexes in Dubai.

item.title: 1973 Battle of the Sexes was about social change - King
item.description: American tennis legend Billie Jean King says the upcoming 'Battle of the Sexes' match between Aryna Sabalenka and Nick Kyrgios is "not the same" as her 1973 victory over Bobby Riggs.

item.title: Sabalenka confident 'Battle of the Sexes' won't damage women's sport
item.description: BBC Sport speaks to Aryna Sabalenka and Nick Kyrgios about their controversial Battle of the Sexes-style match, set to take place in Dubai on 28 December.

item.title: Women's tennis going in right direction - King
item.description: Billie Jean King, founder of the WTA, believes the organisation's billion-dollar deal with Mercedes shows the tour "continues to lead the way" in women's sport.

item.title: Boulter loses to Pegula as GB out of BJK Cup
item.description: Watch the best shots, as the United States' Jessica Pegula fights back from a set down to beat Katie Boulter and knock Great Britain out of the Billie Jean King Cup at the semi-final stage.

item.title: GB's Kartal runs Navarro close in BJK Cup semi-final
item.description: A battling Sonay Kartal falls to the United States' Emma Navarro, as Great Britain go 1-0 down in the semi-finals of the Billie Jean King Cup.

item.title: Boulter win puts GB into BJK Cup semi-finals
item.description: Watch the best shots, as Katie Boulter's win over Moyuka Uchijima sees Great Britain secure a 2-0 win over Japan, setting up a Billie Jean King Cup semi-final with the United States.

item.title: Who has won tennis' 'Battle of the Sexes'?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team details the history of 'Battle of the Sexes' matches in tennis

item.title: What is US Open prize money?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at how much prize money is on offer at the US Open.

item.title: Why is there a pineapple on Wimbledon's men's trophy?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at why there is a pineapple on the top of the Wimbledon trophy for the winner of the men's singles.

item.title: Why were there no line judges at Wimbledon 2025?
item.description: Wimbledon are relying on electronic line calling to judge whether a ball is in or out.
